revision.text:
[[ja:岩屋妙見宮]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 1.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Mitoyo|qid=Q873578|1=Mitoyo, Kagawa}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.21838888888889|N|133.64480555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |name = Iwaya Myōken-gū|native_name = 岩屋妙見宮 |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 1.jpg|260px]] |address = [[香川県]][[三豊市]]仁尾町仁尾戊1107 | 緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 13 | 緯度秒 = 06.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 133 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP-36 |山号 = 七寶山 |院号 = |寺号 = 岩屋妙見宮 |宗派 = [[真言宗醍醐派]] |本尊 = [[妙見菩薩|北辰妙見大菩薩]] |創建年 =　不詳 |開基 = [[空海]] |正式名 = 七寶山 吉祥院 奥之院 岩屋妙見宮 |別称 = 朝日の妙見さん |札所等 = |文化財 = 吉祥院棟札（三豊市指定文化財）
妙見遺跡（三豊市指定文化財） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa Prefecture|label=岩屋妙見宮|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Iwaya Myōken-gū''' (岩屋妙見宮) is a {{ill|Dō (architecture)|en|Dō (architecture)|qid=Q11377515|lt=Buddhist hall|1=Dō (architecture)}} (Okunoin) of {{ill|Jippāramitsu-ji|ja|十波羅蜜寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jippāramitsu-ji|qid=Q135579307|1=Q135579307}} (Kisshō-in) located in Asahi, {{ill|Mitoyo, Kagawa|en|Mitoyo, Kagawa|qid=Q873578|lt=Mitoyo, Kagawa|1=Mitoyo, Kagawa}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|qid=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}. The principal deity is {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}, specifically revered as '''Hokushin Myōken Daibosatsu''' (北辰妙見大菩薩). The temple is situated on the mid-slope of {{ill|Mount Myōken|en|Mount Myōken|qid=Q868348|lt=Mount Myōken|1=Mount Myōken}}[https://www.city.mitoyo.lg.jp/bunka/midokoro/5/3787.html Mitoyo City Official Website] and is affectionately known as '''"Asahi no Myōken-san"''' (朝日の妙見さん). == History == It is said that {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} (Kōbō Daishi) secluded himself at this mountain for ascetic training, practicing the '''Gumonjihō''' mantra. During his meditation, Myōken Bosatsu appeared to him in a vision and proclaimed: *"I am the incarnation of the North Star. I entrust myself to you so that you may bring great fortune to the people of the future. You must carve my image here and establish a temple."* Following this revelation, Kūkai carved the '''Kokūzō Bosatsu''' (the original form of Myōken) into the rock face and established a temple at the siteBased on the local information board.. In '''1691 (Genroku 4)''', '''Jōzō''' of Kisshō-in, with contributions from '''Shioda Denzōemon''' and others, reconstructed the principal image and temple hall (as recorded in one of the '''47 temple plaques of Kisshō-in''', designated a '''Mitoyo City cultural property''' on October 26, 1978). Additionally, '''ritual vessels from approximately 1,200 years ago''' were excavated from the eastern mountain slope near the main hall, leading to the site's designation as the '''Myōken Ruins''' in 1973. == Temple Complex == * '''Main Hall (Hon-dō)''': A unique '''Myōken-dō''' featuring a '''natural rock ceiling''' covered by a '''stone canopy'''. * '''Daishi Hall (Daishi-dō)''': Houses a '''stone statue of Yakuyoke Daishi (protective Kūkai figure)''', donated in '''1855''', and reconstructed in '''1986'''. * '''Gumonji Hall (Gumonji-dō)''': Enshrines the '''stone statue of Kokūzō Bosatsu''', said to have been carved by Kūkai himself. * '''Kannon Hall (Kannon-dō)''': The '''first stop''' on a miniature '''Saigoku 33 Kannon pilgrimage'''. * '''Retreat Hall (Komori-dō)''': A hall for '''overnight meditation'''. * '''Cave of Fortune (Kaiun no Dōketsu)''': An entrance near the '''right side of the main hall''', leading through darkness to an exit near the '''stone canopy'''. (Currently closed to visitors.) File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 2.jpg|Torii gate and stone staircase File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 3.jpg|Natural rock ceiling of the main hall File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 4.jpg|Entrance to the cave File:Iwaya myoukendou 20221225 5.jpg|Exit from the cave == Festivals == * '''New Year's First Visit (Shōgatsu Hatsumairi)''' * '''April's Second Weekend Festival''' * '''Summer Festival (Summer Matsuri)''' – Held on the '''second weekend of July'''. == Access == [[File:Iwaya myoukengu 20221225 2.jpg|thumb|Scenic view from the temple grounds]] * From '''Prefectural Route 231 (Nio-Takuma Line)''', take the '''forest road near Mitoyo Fureai Plaza'''. * Parking is available near the '''torii gate''' (space for approximately three cars). * A '''10-minute climb up the stone steps''' leads to the temple grounds. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|岩屋妙見宮|version=97943195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:岩屋妙見宮]] [[da:d:Q116946767]] {{wikidata link|Q116946767}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Mitoyo, Kagawa]] [[Category:Municipal History of Mitoyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
